title: Building a Full-Stack App with Zero Config
content:
I used to spend hours setting up build tools, configuring bundlers, and wiring together separate frontend and backend repos. Not anymore.

The Old Way

Remember when building a full-stack app meant:

  • Setting up Express or Django for the backend
  • Configuring Webpack or Vite for the frontend
  • Managing CORS and proxy settings
  • Deploying two separate services

The New Way

With Next.js App Router, everything lives in one project:

app/
  page.js          # frontend
  api/
    users/
      route.js     # backend API

API routes are just files. No server setup, no routing configuration. You export a function and it works.

Database Integration

Server components can query databases directly:

async function getUsers() {
  const db = await connectDB();
  return db.users.findMany();
}

No API calls, no serialization overhead. Just direct database access from your components.

This is the developer experience I always wanted — less config, more building.
